message:
test(fnp-python): metamorphic fuzz-style invariants on cod-ported ops

Adds `metamorphic_rust_ported_ops_preserve_invariants` — a
property-based smoke test covering 9 algebraic invariants that cod's
rust-native implementations MUST preserve. Per
/testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 4 this complements per-op parity
tests (which only check output vs numpy) with INTERNAL consistency
checks that would expose edge-case bugs numpy oracle alone cannot catch.

Invariants exercised:

1. flip∘flip = identity (axis=0, for 1-D, 2-D, 3-D arrays)
2. transpose∘transpose = identity (2-D + 3-D)
3. fliplr∘fliplr = identity (2-D)
4. flipud∘flipud = identity (2-D + 3-D)
5. rot90(k=2) applied twice = identity (2-D)
6. concatenate([x]) = x (1-element identity)
7. diff(cumsum(x)) ≈ x[1:] (off-by-one + sign check)
8. sort + flip(axis=0) = numpy's descending sort
9. argsort(argsort(x)) applied to sort(x) ≈ x (permutation inverse)

Inputs: 1-D float array, 2-D int matrix, 3-D float tensor — hitting
all three rank branches in cod's rust-ports to flush out any
shape-specific bugs in the iteration / stride / axis-normalisation
paths.

RESULT: all 9 invariants hold. No HIGH-level bugs found in:
- flip / flipud / fliplr (cod commit 316576e: rust-port flip family)
- transpose (cod commit 2f2d773: rust-port transpose axis family)
- rot90 (same family)
- sort / argsort
- cumsum / diff
- concatenate

Verified via:
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib metamorphic_rust_ported_ops
1 passed, 0 failed, finished in 0.11s.

This is the third consecutive review cycle (k74v.8 adversarial ×2 +
metamorphic fuzz) yielding zero HIGH/CRITICAL divergences. The
fnp-python layer + cod's rust-ports are behaviourally solid under
adversarial + metamorphic probing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
